title: WIN-021 (gyroscopic precession) — close τ/I-framing defender escape with quantitative magnitude falsification
target: data/wins.json ["021"].detail_evidence — replace single sentence acknowledging the rad/s² defender retreat with four-sentence response that quantitatively closes the τ = Iα framing escape using IAU 2006 P_03 secular acceleration as the empirical bound. Single-item EXP addressing tracker entry EXP-326 (ISS-1851, BAU moderate).
kernel_of_truth: The τ = Iα framing is real classical mechanics. Gyroscopic systems under applied torques do have meaningful angular accelerations, and Earth precession does have a real secular acceleration component (the t² coefficient in IAU 2006 P_03). The dome got the dimensional form (rad/s²) of a physically meaningful quantity — but the magnitude is off by ~12 orders of magnitude, and the dome's own text labels the quantity as a rate, not an acceleration. We acknowledge the framing is intelligible, then close it on magnitude and on dome-side framing.
summary_for_decider: Single-patch verification EXP closing ISS-1851 (WIN-021.c3 moderate hole). The current detail_evidence acknowledges a defender retreat ("rad/s² is intentional — meant as acceleration") with a qualitative argument about VLBI seeing precession rate as constant. The curmudgeon found this leaves the τ = Iα framing open: a defender could still argue α is a meaningful instantaneous physical quantity even if the precession axis appears kinematically constant on short timescales. The patch replaces that one sentence with four sentences that (1) acknowledge the τ/I framing as real classical mechanics — kernel of truth, (2) quantitatively falsify by comparing 4.87×10⁻¹² rad/s² to the IAU 2006 P_03 secular acceleration of ≈10⁻²⁴ rad/s² (twelve orders of magnitude too large), (3) note the runaway consequence (×2×10⁷ Earth's rate per year), and (4) point out the dome's own page text frames the claim as a rate, not an acceleration — the reframe is a defender retreat the published claim does not actually make. Verdict (Misleading) does not change; this is content strengthening only.

patches.old_string: A defender might argue the rad/s² is intentional (predicting precession acceleration, not rate) — but VLBI and lunar laser ranging measure precession rate as effectively constant on human timescales, so a specific angular-acceleration value would imply a detectable changing precession rate that neither historical data nor modern geodesy records.
patches.new_string: A defender might argue the rad/s² is intentional — that under a τ = Iα framing, a specific angular-acceleration value is meaningful as the second derivative of the precession angle driven by luni-solar torques. The escape fails on magnitude: Earth's observed secular acceleration of luni-solar precession is captured in the IAU 2006 P_03 model as the t² coefficient of the longitudinal expression (≈1 arcsec/century²), which converts to roughly 10⁻²⁴ rad/s² — about twelve orders of magnitude smaller than the dome's 4.87×10⁻¹² rad/s². If the dome's number were a real angular acceleration, integrating it over a single year would multiply Earth's actual precession rate by a factor of ~2×10⁷, a runaway that VLBI and lunar laser ranging would have caught decades ago. The dome's own page text frames this as a precession rate matching the 25,772-year cycle (a velocity-class quantity, the same type as the IAU 2006 value), so the acceleration reading is a defender retreat the published claim does not actually make.
patches.rationale: Closes the τ = Iα defender retreat with quantitative magnitude bound (IAU 2006 P_03 secular term ≈10⁻²⁴ rad/s² vs claimed 4.87×10⁻¹² rad/s², twelve orders of magnitude). Also flags the integrated-over-one-year runaway consequence (×2×10⁷) and the dome-side framing-mismatch (page text says "rate," not "acceleration"). Verdict unchanged (Misleading). Net evidence length grows from one to four sentences in this section; total detail_evidence grows by ~500 chars.
patches.verification: After integration: (a) grep "IAU 2006 P_03" data/wins.json should return exactly one hit in WIN-021. (b) grep "twelve orders of magnitude" data/wins.json should return one hit in WIN-021. (c) Existing tests in tests/run.sh should pass unchanged — no numeric data values touched, only verdict-section prose.

anticipated_objections.objection: The IAU 2006 P_03 t² coefficient depends on which expression (longitudinal ψ_A vs obliquity ε_A vs general precession) — citing "~1 arcsec/century²" without specifying could be challenged.
anticipated_objections.response: We use ≈ and "of order" framing; the precise t² coefficient varies between 1 and 2 arcsec/century² across IAU 2006 P_03 expressions (e.g., Capitaine et al. 2003 gives 1.07946 arcsec/century² for ψ_A, others give larger). Even at 2× the cited value, the conversion to rad/s² is still ≈2×10⁻²⁴ — twelve orders of magnitude is robust to factor-of-few uncertainty in the coefficient.

anticipated_objections.objection: The defender could pivot from "precession acceleration" to "Chandler wobble acceleration" or "polar wander acceleration" or some other angular second derivative.
anticipated_objections.response: Pivoting to a different physical quantity at defense time is exactly the post-hoc retreat that the trivial-transcription / dome-monitor-labels-it-rate argument blocks. The dome's text frames the claim as a precession rate matching Earth's 25,772-year cycle; any retreat to a different physical α (Chandler ≈10⁻¹⁴ rad/s², polar wander even smaller) abandons the original framing. The new sentence explicitly notes this.

anticipated_objections.objection: The phrase "runaway that VLBI and lunar laser ranging would have caught decades ago" needs a more specific bound — what is the actual observational upper limit on second-derivative precession?
anticipated_objections.response: IERS Earth Orientation Parameters (EOP-C04 series) constrain the precession rate to ~7 significant figures over decades. The implied bound on instantaneous α is conservatively below 10⁻²² rad/s² and likely tighter; the IAU 2006 P_03 t² coefficient of order 10⁻²⁴ rad/s² is the encoded secular value. Either bound forces a margin of ≥10¹⁰ between observed-limit and dome-claimed α. The verdict-text framing — "would have caught decades ago" — is qualitative and conservative; we don't need a tighter quantitative bound to make the point.

new_evidence_added: IAU 2006 P_03 precession model — secular t² coefficient as quantitative bound on Earth precession's angular acceleration (~1 arcsec/century² ≈ 10⁻²⁴ rad/s²)

Runaway consequence calculation: ∫α dt over one year = 1.54×10⁻⁴ rad/s; ratio to actual precession rate (7.73×10⁻¹² rad/s) ≈ 2×10⁷ — i.e., if the dome's α were real, Earth precession rate would multiply by 20 million per year, observable in any modern geodesy series
holes_addressed: WIN-021.c3 moderate hole: τ = Iα framing defender escape was acknowledged but not quantitatively closed. Now closed on magnitude (12 OOM) and on framing (dome text says "rate", not "acceleration").
test_coverage: No new numeric tests required — the IAU 2006 P_03 coefficient is a textbook physics value, and the dome's 4.87×10⁻¹² rad/s² is already tested implicitly via existing wins.json content tests. The new prose contains one new quantitative claim (≈10⁻²⁴ rad/s²) which traces directly to IAU 2006 P_03 — recommend adding a footnote citation if curmudgeon wants tighter sourcing, but not strictly needed since the IAU model name is itself the citation.
notes: Single-item run per analyst.md "work one item per run" guidance. The other 14 pending tracker items remain for subsequent runs; the 137 PROP-029 orphans remain flagged via the prior run's attention-inbox notice (PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED-2026-05-13) for tinker investigation.
